title: Build /flow evolve foundation (Rung 2: Steps 1-3)
description: Turn the Rung-1 one-shot audit into real machinery per the approved plan (~/.claude/plans/but-wait-the-curious-puppy.md). Step 1: tracker_cli.py create subcommand (flow_beads_create wrapper; BEADS_DIR targeting; refuse non-Flow targets). Step 2: reflect sling-bead on the propose+record path, gated on maintainer mode + recall filter for MACHINERY entries. Step 3: /flow evolve cold-audit verb (router row + references/verb-evolve.md) with stable evidence-hash bead IDs + closed/rejected memory so it converges. Maintainer detection: committed marker in .flow/workspace.toml, NOT an env var. Defer cloud shipper (Steps 4-5) until Rung 3 PR quality proven — now proven (PRs #8-#11). Open wrinkles: committed-marker-vs-fork contradiction; verify machinery_edit targets dev repo vs installed copy.

title: Jira adapter write/mutation methods have zero test coverage (create, link, all setters, board_rank, attachments)
description:
EVIDENCE:
tracker_jira.py defines create (554), set_summary (580), set_description (588), set_priority (591), set_labels (594), set_assignee (597), link (649), board_rank (819), get/upload_attachments, set_components, set_custom_field. Verified: grep for `.create(`/`.link(`/`.set_summary(` in test_jira_adapter.py = 0; the 42 tests cover only reads/transitions/sprint/watcher/fix-versions/epic-link. The beads equivalents ARE tested (test_beads_adapter.py:333,353,365 call adapter.create). These methods build branching REST payloads (create: plain-vs-ADF summary, optional parent/labels/assignee at 563-577), so a payload-shape regression ships silently to live Jira.

VALUE / RATIONALE:
Jira write paths are how /flow mutates real tickets; an untested PUT/POST payload that Jira rejects or mis-applies corrupts tracker state for paying users, and the asymmetry means the cheaper backend (beads) is the verified one. High value, strong evidence — but many methods, so not 'small/isolated'; ranks below the one-liners. Touches only the test file.

BLAST RADIUS: plugins/flow/skills/flow/scripts/tests/test_jira_adapter.py
HOT FILE: False
AUDIT SOURCES: tests-coverage mining
AUDIT ID: jira-write-methods-untested

title: pending-mutations queue is built + tested but no adapter or stage ever enqueues; transient tracker mutations are silently lost
description:
EVIDENCE:
Verified: append_mutation has zero non-test, non-self callers (grep of append_mutation across *.py returns only its definition at pending_mutations.py:152, a docstring, and the __all__ export). Only the drain side is wired: sync.py:87 list_mutations, :101 compact. tracker_beads.py and tracker_jira.py contain zero 'pending' references. The commit-stage transient-failure branch (stage-commit.md:94-97) logs a warning and continues — it drops the mutation, never enqueues. sync.py:3 and verb-sync-baseline.md assert the file is 'written by adapter failure paths' — false for both adapters. This is the unaddressed core of the stale TODO at tracker_beads.py:19-23 ('TODO (phase 8): transient-failure paths should append to pending-mutations.jsonl ... until phase 8 ships'), which contradicts dev-history.md (phase 8d shipped /flow sync) — fold the stale-TODO finding in here, it is the breadcrumb for the same unwired enqueue.

VALUE / RATIONALE:
Highest value of the set: in unattended runs a network/auth blip on a tracker transition (or comment/link/edit) is dropped permanently and /flow sync can never reconcile it because nothing wrote it. The whole pending-mutations subsystem (module + CLI + drain + verb prose) is dead weight until a write path is wired in. BUT: blast-radius-safety pulls this down — wiring append_mutation into the commit/ticket transient-failure branches touches wired adapters (tracker_beads/jira) and stage-commit.md, so it is NOT a safe small ship. Rank high on value, flag the tradeoff. Touches wired handlers = hot.

BLAST RADIUS: plugins/flow/skills/flow/scripts/tracker_beads.py, plugins/flow/skills/flow/scripts/tracker_jira.py, plugins/flow/skills/flow/references/stage-commit.md, plugins/flow/skills/flow/references/stage-ticket.md, plugins/flow/skills/flow/scripts/pending_mutations.py
HOT FILE: True
AUDIT SOURCES: friction-history mining
AUDIT ID: pending-mutations-never-enqueued

title: Flow self-evolution: Rung 1 audit backlog
description:
Backlog from the Rung 1 cold self-audit of the flow repo (see plan: Flow self-evolution proactive nightly loop).

The premise holds: this cold self-audit produced a genuine, non-noise yield. The shippable-as-small-tickets head is real — ty-skips-hooks (one-token fix, every-session blast), inventory.md --labels/--set-labels (one-line, ironclad), git quotePath (concrete correctness bug, confirmed 0 quotePath calls), the missing-stage-docs fix+guard pair, and the pending-mutations dead-enqueue (highest value, though it touches wired adapters so not a safe-small ship). Below that sits a legitimate but low-stakes housekeeping tail (duplicated _parse_iso/_atomicio/Runner helpers, complexity hotspots, stale comments, perms-narrowing). All top claims were re-verified empirically (lint task omits hooks, MODULE.md has 0 heartbeat refs, both registry stage docs MISSING, append_mutation has no enqueue caller, set_labels uses --set-labels, 0 quotePath in diff_extract, jira write methods have 0 test refs). Net: self-evolution mining works; ship the high-evidence head, batch the tail.

Every child carries file:line / reproduced-command evidence. Label `evolve`. The small leaf head ships as draft PRs first (Rung 3).

title: ticket stage: claim tracker backend (open->in_progress) to prevent fleet double-pick
description: The ticket stage (references/stage-ticket.md, handler=inline) stamps .flow/tickets/<KEY>.md frontmatter status=in_progress but never transitions the tracker BACKEND. On beads the bd issue stays 'open' for the whole run, so 'bd ready' still lists the in-flight ticket as available and a parallel fleet agent could double-pick it. Fix: add a best-effort backend transition (open->in_progress) to the ticket stage, mirroring the stage-commit transition step (MCP-first, REST/tracker_cli fallback), tolerant of exit 3 (no transition available / already in_progress) and transient faults so it never fails the run. Surfaced on flow-7qm with the machinery lens ON (PROPOSE+RECORD).

title: sync._invoke comment/link/edit op branches + unknown-op fallthrough never exercised through reconcile()
description:
EVIDENCE:
sync.py:_invoke (63-79) dispatches on op in {transition, comment, link, edit} plus a final `return False`. All 3 tests in test_sync.py seed pending mutations with op='transition' only (lines 43,59,74). The fake _Tracker stubs comment/link/edit (25-31) but no test routes those through reconcile(), so 3 dispatch branches + the unknown-op guard stay unexecuted.

VALUE / RATIONALE:
The pending-mutation drain is load-bearing safety; 3 of its 4 op handlers plus the unknown-op guard are untested, so a malformed link/edit replay could throw or silently no-op uncaught. Cheap to close (fixture already exists). Distinct from the dead-enqueue finding. Touches one test file.

BLAST RADIUS: plugins/flow/skills/flow/scripts/tests/test_sync.py
HOT FILE: False
AUDIT SOURCES: tests-coverage mining
AUDIT ID: sync-nontransition-op-branches-untested

title: iter_jsonl re-quarantines the same malformed line on every read; sidecar grows unbounded
description:
EVIDENCE:
_jsonl.py:30-52 iter_jsonl appends each bad line to the quarantine sidecar and explicitly never rewrites the main file. pending_mutations.py:133 calls iter_jsonl on every append (dedup scan) and :209 on every compact; recall paths call it on every recall. Because the offending line stays in the main file, one corrupt line in pending-mutations.jsonl or knowledge.jsonl appends one new sidecar record per call, forever, and is reprocessed each time.

VALUE / RATIONALE:
Unbounded disk growth and repeated wasted work from a single corrupt line, on a workspace that ever sees one malformed JSONL line. The append-only main-file invariant is correct and load-bearing, so the fix is dedup at the sidecar (track already-quarantined raw lines per pass, or key by content hash) — do not rewrite the source. Isolated to _jsonl.py.

BLAST RADIUS: plugins/flow/skills/flow/scripts/_jsonl.py
HOT FILE: False
AUDIT SOURCES: robustness-review
AUDIT ID: jsonl-quarantine-unbounded-growth

title: Atomic-write helper reimplemented 4x weaker than shared _atomicio (dropped dir-fsync + temp cleanup)
description:
EVIDENCE:
Verified: `def _atomic_write` defined in diff_extract.py, init.py, state.py, ticket_frontmatter.py — separate from the shared _atomicio.atomic_write_bytes (imported by 8 modules). Canonical does temp+fsync+os.replace plus (a) parent-dir fsync for crash-durable rename, (b) temp unlink on BaseException. The four local copies stop at os.replace with no dir-fsync and use NamedTemporaryFile(delete=False), leaking the .tmp on mid-write exception. state.py added the dir-fsync back (proving the gap was noticed once) but still leaks the temp — so the duplication has already drifted: one of four hand-patched, three not.

VALUE / RATIONALE:
Restoring these call sites to the shared helper recovers parent-dir fsync (rename durability across crash) and temp cleanup the copies dropped — aligns with the load-bearing 'atomic writes + quarantine' invariant rather than eroding it. Low real-world trigger (crash/mid-write only) but it is exactly the robustness machinery the project calls load-bearing, and the drift means future safety edits must repeat 5 places. Mechanical, reviewable.

BLAST RADIUS: plugins/flow/skills/flow/scripts/diff_extract.py, plugins/flow/skills/flow/scripts/init.py, plugins/flow/skills/flow/scripts/ticket_frontmatter.py, plugins/flow/skills/flow/scripts/state.py, plugins/flow/skills/flow/scripts/_atomicio.py
HOT FILE: False
AUDIT SOURCES: dead-code-complexity mining
AUDIT ID: atomic-write-reimplemented-weaker

title: No test asserts every registry reference_doc resolves to a real file; seam_check is blind to it
description:
EVIDENCE:
test_registry.py (37 lines) only checks load/by-name/malformed; never iterates entries asserting Path(reference_doc).is_file() (grep of is_file|exists|reference_doc returns only the REAL_REGISTRY path constant). validate_workspace.py has no reference_doc existence check. seam_check.py validates only ${CLAUDE_SKILL_DIR}/scripts/<x>.py invocations against argparse — it ran green (exit 0) while two registry docs are missing. So registry-missing-stage-docs is invisible to all three guards (lint, seam_check, suite).

VALUE / RATIONALE:
The regression net that would have caught the missing-docs drift at commit time. A short parametrized test over load_registry(REAL_REGISTRY) asserting (SKILL_ROOT / e.reference_doc).is_file() for every entry closes the seam_check blind spot for the registry→doc edge. Ship as the guard pairing with registry-missing-stage-docs. Touches only a test file.

BLAST RADIUS: plugins/flow/skills/flow/scripts/tests/test_registry.py
HOT FILE: False
AUDIT SOURCES: flow architecture-seam mining
AUDIT ID: test-registry-doc-existence

title: Registry points at two reference docs that do not exist (stage-create_pr.md, stage-review_loop.md)
description:
EVIDENCE:
stage-registry.toml:128/142 set reference_doc to references/stage-create_pr.md and stage-review_loop.md; verified both MISSING (`ls references/stage-*.md` returns only code_review/commit/e2e/implement/plan/reflect/ticket; test -f confirms both absent; git log empty — never committed). dispatch_stage.py emits reference_doc unconditionally. Both default_handler=none so the bare happy path skips the Read, keeping it latent — but reconfiguring either to inline/subagent via the init wizard hits SKILL.md's 'Read ${CLAUDE_SKILL_DIR}/${descriptor.reference_doc}' on a nonexistent path and the stage breaks. e2e is also default-none yet ships stage-e2e.md for exactly this case; create_pr/review_loop do not (the asymmetry is the tell). Folds in: SKILL.md routing section (lines ~191-205) also omits create_pr/review_loop, camouflaging the dangling entries.

VALUE / RATIONALE:
Silently violates the registry's stated one-entry-one-doc contract (stage-registry.toml header). Latent today but a hard failure the moment a workspace opts into a PR-creating/review inline handler — the exact reconfigure path the registry advertises. Fix: write the two docs mirroring stage-e2e.md, or drop reference_doc from entries that genuinely never need one (and align the SKILL.md routing note). Touches stage-registry.toml/SKILL.md = hot.

BLAST RADIUS: plugins/flow/skills/flow/references/stage-create_pr.md, plugins/flow/skills/flow/references/stage-review_loop.md, plugins/flow/skills/flow/stage-registry.toml, plugins/flow/skills/flow/SKILL.md
HOT FILE: True
AUDIT SOURCES: flow architecture-seam mining
AUDIT ID: registry-missing-stage-docs

title: inventory.md beads contract table documents wrong flag: `bd update --labels` but code calls `--set-labels`
description:
EVIDENCE:
inventory.md `bd update <key>` row lists `--labels`; tracker_beads.py:487 calls `_run(["update", key, "--set-labels", joined])` and its own comment (line 484) says bd update has --set-labels/--add-label. Verified both: the update row says --labels, the code uses --set-labels. (bd create DOES use --labels at tracker_beads.py:450, matching the create row — drift is specific to the update row.)

VALUE / RATIONALE:
Wrong value in the authoritative beads-CLI contract table, not a gap. Anyone (human or the reflect/self-evolution loop) consulting it to build a `bd update` labels invocation emits `--labels`, which bd rejects. One-line doc fix, ironclad evidence.

BLAST RADIUS: plugins/flow/skills/flow/scripts/inventory.md
HOT FILE: False
AUDIT SOURCES: docs-drift mining
AUDIT ID: inventory-bd-update-labels-flag-wrong

title: git status/ls-files/diff parsed without core.quotePath=false: special-char paths break the ownership gate
description:
EVIDENCE:
diff_extract.py:323 check_ownership parses `git status --porcelain --untracked-files=all` via `line[3:].strip()`; :132 _untracked_files parses `git ls-files`; :153 diff_since parses `git diff --numstat` — all via splitlines, none pass core.quotePath=false (verified: grep -c quotePath diff_extract.py = 0). Reproduced in scratch repo: default git emits `?? "sub/n\303\251w.py"` (quoted, C-escaped); with `core.quotePath false` emits literal `sub/néw.py`. A planned file named `weird name.py` parses as the quoted string and never matches the owned set, flagging a legit file as unowned.

VALUE / RATIONALE:
Correctness/availability bug in the load-bearing content-ownership commit gate (stage-implement.md:36, stage-commit.md). Any planned filename with a space, non-ASCII byte, quote, or backslash gets mis-classified and can block a legit commit. Not a safety bypass (quoted string never goes permissive). Fix is one line at three call sites: `git -c core.quotePath=false ...` or `-z` + NUL split. diff_extract is safety-sensitive but not a wired stage handler.

BLAST RADIUS: plugins/flow/skills/flow/scripts/diff_extract.py
HOT FILE: False
AUDIT SOURCES: robustness-review
AUDIT ID: git-porcelain-quotepath-parsing

title: ty type-check omits hooks/ despite lint task description claiming coverage
description:
EVIDENCE:
mise.toml [tasks.lint] description says 'ty check (scripts + plugin hooks)' but run list is `ruff check . ../../../hooks`, `ruff format --check . ../../../hooks`, `ty check .` — the ty step is bare `ty check .` (scripts only). Verified: the two ruff steps include ../../../hooks, ty does not. CI runs `mise run lint` from the scripts dir, so hooks/session-start.py (276 lines, fully annotated) is never type-checked. Running `ty check ../../../hooks` passes today, proving it is an omission not a deliberate exclusion.

VALUE / RATIONALE:
The SessionStart recall hook is wired into every session; a future type regression there (wrong dict shape, None deref) ships green because ty never sees the file. Fix is one token (`ty check . ../../../hooks`). Poster-child candidate: verified, isolated, every-session blast, near-zero review cost.

BLAST RADIUS: plugins/flow/skills/flow/scripts/mise.toml
HOT FILE: False
AUDIT SOURCES: quality-gates mining
AUDIT ID: ty-check-skips-hooks

title: throwaway transition probe
init.py: pass --skip-agents --non-interactive to bd init
description: flow's init.py runs bare 'bd init --prefix <prefix>' (init.py:498). bd init then generates AGENTS.md and .claude/settings.json (a bd prime SessionStart/PreCompact hook) and auto-commits them — invasive for a shared, Claude-Code-only repo. bd has '--skip-agents' (help: 'Skip AGENTS.md and Claude settings generation') which suppresses both at the source, and '--non-interactive' for headless/background safety (flow often runs detached). Fix: add both flags to the bd init invocation in _run_bd_init. Consider --skip-hooks too (decide whether flow wants bd's export-on-commit git hooks). Surfaced while dogfooding flow on flow.
acceptance_criteria: bd init invoked with --skip-agents --non-interactive; a fresh /flow init backend=beads produces no AGENTS.md and no .claude/settings.json; existing init tests updated for the new argv.

title: Gate tests/ under the ty type checker
description: The ty type gate currently excludes tests/ (set in scripts/pyproject.toml [tool.ty.src] exclude = ["tests"]). Bring tests under the gate: add [tool.ty.analysis] allowed-unresolved-imports = ["pytest"] (32 of 52 test diagnostics are just pytest being unresolvable in ty's env), drop tests from the exclude (keep tests/_fixtures excluded), then triage and fix the ~20 real test type diagnostics. Success: mise run lint passes with tests included, and a deliberately-broken annotation in a test file makes the gate exit 1.
acceptance_criteria: mise run lint green with tests type-checked; broken annotation in a test file fails the gate; full pytest suite still passes.

title: No coverage instrumentation in dev tooling — untested-public-method gaps are invisible to CI
description:
EVIDENCE:
`import coverage` and `import pytest_cov` both fail (ModuleNotFoundError) in system python3 AND the mise dev venv; mise.toml `mise run test` runs plain pytest with no --cov. The three method-level gaps (jira writes, sync branches, beads set_description) pass CI green at 826 tests because nothing measures executed lines — they were only found by manual method-vs-test grep.

VALUE / RATIONALE:
Adding pytest-cov as a dev dep + a `mise run cov` task (or a CI floor) makes exactly this class of untested-public-method drift visible automatically instead of relying on manual audits. Meta/tooling — enables the other test-gap tickets but lower direct value. Touches dev config only.

BLAST RADIUS: plugins/flow/skills/flow/scripts/mise.toml, plugins/flow/skills/flow/scripts/pyproject.toml
HOT FILE: False
AUDIT SOURCES: tests-coverage mining
AUDIT ID: no-coverage-instrumentation

title: Beads adapter set_description (with postcondition re-read) is the lone untested setter
description:
EVIDENCE:
tracker_beads.py:469-475 set_description runs `bd update --description`, checks returncode, then calls _verify_field(...) which re-reads and can raise. grep for set_description in test_beads_adapter.py = 0. The sibling setters set_summary/set_priority/set_labels ARE tested with postcondition re-read verification, so set_description is the lone gap in an otherwise verified setter family.

VALUE / RATIONALE:
Cheap to close (the sibling-setter fixture pattern already exists) and guards the _verify_field round-trip that distinguishes a real bd write from a silent no-op. Touches one test file. Low stakes.

BLAST RADIUS: plugins/flow/skills/flow/scripts/tests/test_beads_adapter.py
HOT FILE: False
AUDIT SOURCES: tests-coverage mining
AUDIT ID: beads-set-description-untested

title: recall.py docstring claims `--metric` is deferred, but the passthrough is implemented
description:
EVIDENCE:
recall.py:19 docstring says '`--metric tickets-per-week` deferred to phase 8d. Mvp = query mode only.' But recall.py:232 `if '--metric' in argv:` intercepts and :235 returns metric.cli_main(...). dev-history.md:14 confirms phase 8d shipped recall.py --metric, and SKILL.md:51 documents it as a live verb. Verified the docstring text in place.

VALUE / RATIONALE:
A comment contradicting the code directly below it (and the shipped feature) misleads anyone reading recall.py to understand the metric path and erodes trust in inline docs. One-line fix.

BLAST RADIUS: plugins/flow/skills/flow/scripts/recall.py
HOT FILE: False
AUDIT SOURCES: docs-drift mining
AUDIT ID: recall-stale-metric-deferred-comment

title: 2-3 functions materially over McCabe 10; ruff gate does not select C901/PLR
description:
EVIDENCE:
ruff C901 (max-complexity=10): tracker_jira.py:290 _request = complexity 20 / 92 lines (HTTP + retry/backoff + error mapping in one body); bundle_discover.py:130 _validate_manifest = 20 / 115 lines (nested _str_list copy-pasted for 4 fields); validate_workspace.py:92 _validate_pipeline_block = 19. pyproject.toml select = [E,F,I,B,UP,SIM,RUF] — C901/PLR0911/0912/0913/0915 NOT selected, so `mise run lint` never flags complexity.

VALUE / RATIONALE:
_request and the two validators are the genuinely hard-to-follow functions; splitting their error-mapping / per-field arms improves the tracker and seam-validation hot paths. Optionally add C901 to the ruff select so the existing gate catches future regressions. Many other c12>10 hits are borderline and not ticket-worthy — only these 2-3. Low priority.

BLAST RADIUS: plugins/flow/skills/flow/scripts/tracker_jira.py, plugins/flow/skills/flow/scripts/bundle_discover.py, plugins/flow/skills/flow/scripts/validate_workspace.py, plugins/flow/skills/flow/scripts/pyproject.toml
HOT FILE: False
AUDIT SOURCES: dead-code-complexity mining
AUDIT ID: complexity-hotspots-unenforced

title: Self-evolution dispatch-indirection caveat (a stage's own prose fix never takes effect on its authoring run) lives only in the knowledge log, not self-evolution.md
description:
EVIDENCE:
knowledge.jsonl entry b7a364cf78aad2b4 (ticket flow-fab): the do-loop reads each inline stage's reference_doc from ${CLAUDE_SKILL_DIR} (the installed checkout) while implement edits the WORKTREE copy, so a self-modifying-stage fix only validates on the NEXT run after merge. This was lived friction (flow-fab's stage-ticket.md step-3b fix ran old prose, left the backend unclaimed). grep of references/self-evolution.md for worktree/installed/skill_dir/reference_doc/'dispatch indirection'/'next run' returns ZERO matches.

VALUE / RATIONALE:
Anyone editing stage-level reference prose mid-run (the explicit self-evolution thesis) expects their fix to validate on the current run and will be surprised. A one-line caveat in self-evolution.md turns lived friction into durable guidance. Cheap, doc-only.

BLAST RADIUS: plugins/flow/skills/flow/references/self-evolution.md
HOT FILE: False
AUDIT SOURCES: friction-history mining
AUDIT ID: self-evolution-dispatch-indirection-undocumented

title: atomic_write_text silently narrows file permissions to 0o600 on every rewrite
description:
EVIDENCE:
_atomicio.py:18 uses tempfile.mkstemp (creates at 0o600) then os.replace, which keeps the temp mode and discards the destination's prior mode. Verified empirically by the miner: a 0o664 file becomes 0o600 after one call. Every safety rewrite flows through here: run.lock (lease.py:391), state.json, snapshot.json (snapshot.py:192-194), pending-mutations.jsonl (pending_mutations.py:215).

VALUE / RATIONALE:
Harmless for single-user workspaces but in a group-/CI-shared workspace a previously group-readable state/lease file becomes owner-only after the first rewrite, breaking a recover/status invoked under another account. Fix: capture destination st_mode before os.replace and re-chmod (fall back 0o644 when absent), the standard atomic-write pattern. Isolated to _atomicio.py.

BLAST RADIUS: plugins/flow/skills/flow/scripts/_atomicio.py
HOT FILE: False
AUDIT SOURCES: robustness-review
AUDIT ID: atomic-write-narrows-perms

title: Runner type alias + _default_runner factory duplicated across 7 modules with two incompatible callable shapes under one name
description:
EVIDENCE:
Runner = Callable[..., CompletedProcess[str]] redefined identically in tracker_beads/diff_extract/branch_ticket/init/recall_pending; flow_worktree.py:47 uses the STRICTER Callable[[list[str], Path], CompletedProcess[str]] under the same name; lease.py:111 binds the same names to a different shape run(args:list[str])->str (check=True, no cwd). _default_runner reimplemented byte-identically in 6 modules. _quarantine_path is a 2-line dup (recall_pending.py:87 vs pending_mutations.py:71).

VALUE / RATIONALE:
Useful signal is the drift: 'Runner' names two incompatible contracts, so a naive 'consolidate to one shared Runner' would break lease.py's typing or loosen flow_worktree's. The 5-6 identical (list[str],Path) copies are safe to hoist; lease.py must stay separate. Worth a ticket so a future consolidation does not blindly merge them. Low stakes.

BLAST RADIUS: plugins/flow/skills/flow/scripts/diff_extract.py, plugins/flow/skills/flow/scripts/branch_ticket.py, plugins/flow/skills/flow/scripts/recall_pending.py, plugins/flow/skills/flow/scripts/flow_worktree.py, plugins/flow/skills/flow/scripts/tracker_beads.py, plugins/flow/skills/flow/scripts/init.py, plugins/flow/skills/flow/scripts/lease.py
HOT FILE: False
AUDIT SOURCES: dead-code-complexity mining
AUDIT ID: runner-alias-duplicated-divergent

title: _parse_iso reimplemented 5 times under one name with three different contracts
description:
EVIDENCE:
Verified: `def _parse_iso` in heartbeat.py, recall_pending.py, lease.py (byte-identical bare fromisoformat), metric.py (adds .replace('Z','+00:00') + AttributeError catch), validate_postmortem.py (OPPOSITE contract — REQUIRES trailing Z, returns None otherwise). All parse engine-written timestamps and every writer emits a trailing Z. On pinned py3.12 bare fromisoformat accepts 'Z' so no live bug, but metric.py's .replace is dead defensive code and validate_postmortem's Z-required branch is a divergent contract.

VALUE / RATIONALE:
Latent maintenance trap, not a current bug: same name, three semantics. A shared helper collapses five copies to one and makes the Z-handling contract explicit and testable once. Low stakes, mechanical.

BLAST RADIUS: plugins/flow/skills/flow/scripts/heartbeat.py, plugins/flow/skills/flow/scripts/recall_pending.py, plugins/flow/skills/flow/scripts/lease.py, plugins/flow/skills/flow/scripts/metric.py, plugins/flow/skills/flow/scripts/validate_postmortem.py
HOT FILE: False
AUDIT SOURCES: dead-code-complexity mining
AUDIT ID: parse-iso-duplicated-divergent

title: No gate enforces MODULE.md completeness vs scripts on disk
description:
EVIDENCE:
The repo gates the prose↔CLI seam (seam_check.py + test_seam_check.py) but nothing checks that every engine script appears in MODULE.md. The heartbeat.py omission is caught by a one-line `comm -23 <(ls *.py|grep -v ^test) <(grep -oE '[a-z_]+\.py' MODULE.md)` yet no test or CI runs it. seam_check validates invocation flags, not doc-map coverage.

VALUE / RATIONALE:
First instance (heartbeat) already shipped; without a guard MODULE.md drifts every time a script is added without a manual table edit. A trivial test (set of *.py minus test_*.py ⊆ names in MODULE.md) turns silent navigation drift into a CI failure. Pairs as the prevention for module-md-refresh. Touches test/check files.

BLAST RADIUS: plugins/flow/skills/flow/scripts/seam_check.py, plugins/flow/skills/flow/scripts/tests/test_seam_check.py
HOT FILE: False

AUDIT ID: module-md-completeness-guard

title: MODULE.md drifted: missing heartbeat.py row + omits download-attachments / check-ownership subcommands + stale flag summaries
description:
EVIDENCE:
Four instances of one drift, all verified. (1) heartbeat.py (11.2K, wired by stage-registry/_registry/state/recover) has 0 references in MODULE.md (grep -c = 0) — the only engine .py absent from the map. (2) tracker_cli row omits `download-attachments` (actual --help set includes it). (3) diff_extract row omits `check-ownership` — the content-ownership commit gate, corroborated by dev-history.md:14 and SKILL.md; a safety subcommand invisible in the map. (4) flow_friction omits --severity, flow_worktree create omits --worktree-path/--copy/--no-mise-trust, resolve_handler omits --search-roots (MODULE.md:3 scopes to 'key flags', so flag omissions are softer than the missing-row/missing-subcommand cases).

VALUE / RATIONALE:
MODULE.md is the CLAUDE.md-designated 'read this to find a script' map for ~24k LOC; a wired stage module and two whole subcommands (one a load-bearing safety gate) being invisible defeats it. Ship as one consolidated refresh pass, not four tickets. Pairs with module-md-completeness-guard. Touches one doc.

BLAST RADIUS: plugins/flow/skills/flow/scripts/MODULE.md
